input.prompt: I want to build a web-based LLM driven chatbot using C# that allows customers to get help with our internal product PDF documentation and Blazor. I want to use Postgres for data storage along with just a file folder for our documentation. Show me your plan before continuing with the implementation.

data.content: I want to build a web-based LLM driven chatbot using C# that allows customers to get help with our internal product PDF documentation and Blazor. I want to use Postgres for data storage along with just a file folder for our documentation. Show me your plan before continuing with the implementation.

Let me first explore the existing repository structure to understand what's already there, then create a plan.

arguments.file_text:
# HelpChatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using **C# / Blazor Server** that lets customers ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. Uses **PostgreSQL** for data storage and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

## Approach: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
We'll extract text from PDFs, chunk it, generate embeddings, store them in Postgres (via `pgvector`), and retrieve relevant chunks at query time to augment LLM prompts.

## Architecture

```
User ⇄ Blazor Server UI ⇄ ChatService ⇄ LLM (OpenAI / Azure OpenAI)
                                ↕
                        EmbeddingService
                                ↕
                      PostgreSQL + pgvector
                                ↕
                      PdfIngestionService ← Docs/*.pdf
```

## Tech Stack
| Component | Technology |
|---|---|
| Framework | .NET 10 / Blazor Server |
| LLM Client | Microsoft.Extensions.AI + OpenAI provider |
| PDF Parsing | UglyToad.PdfPig |
| Embeddings | OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` |
| Vector Store | PostgreSQL + pgvector (via Npgsql) |
| ORM / DB | Npgsql + Dapper (lightweight) |
| UI | Blazor Server components |

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & NuGet packages
Add required NuGet packages: `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI`, `UglyToad.PdfPig`, `Npgsql`, `Dapper`, Blazor Server services.

### 2. Database schema & setup
- Create a migration/init SQL script for Postgres:
  - `pgvector` extension
  - `document_chunks` table: `id`, `file_name`, `chunk_index`, `content` (text), `embedding` (vector(1536)), `created_at`
  - `chat_history` table: `id`, `session_id`, `role`, `content`, `created_at`

### 3. PDF ingestion service
- `PdfIngestionService`: reads all PDFs from `Docs/` folder
- Extracts text page-by-page using PdfPig
- Splits text into overlapping chunks (~500 tokens, ~100 token overlap)
- Generates embeddings via OpenAI
- Upserts chunks + embeddings into `document_chunks`

### 4. Embedding & retrieval service
- `EmbeddingService`: wraps the OpenAI embeddings API
- `RetrievalService`: takes a user query, generates its embedding, performs cosine-similarity search against `document_chunks` in Postgres, returns top-K relevant chunks

### 5. Chat service (RAG orchestration)
- `ChatService`: orchestrates the RAG flow
  - Accepts user message
  - Calls `RetrievalService` to get relevant context
  - Builds a system prompt with retrieved chunks
  - Sends conversation + context to LLM via `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`
  - Persists conversation in `chat_history`

### 6. Blazor Server UI
- Add Blazor Server services to `Program.cs`
- `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` — app shell
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` — chat page with:
  - Scrollable message list (user/assistant bubbles)
  - Text input + send button
  - Loading indicator while awaiting response
- `_Imports.razor`, `App.razor`, `Routes.razor` — standard Blazor scaffolding
- Basic CSS for chat appearance (in `wwwroot/css/`)

### 7. Configuration & Program.cs wiring
- `appsettings.json`: Postgres connection string, OpenAI API key, docs folder path, model settings
- Register all services in DI
- Add app middleware (static files, antiforgery, map Razor components)

### 8. Admin: document re-ingestion endpoint
- Add a simple `/admin/ingest` page or button that triggers re-ingestion of the `Docs/` folder so new PDFs can be loaded without restarting.

## Notes
- **OpenAI API key** is required — the app reads it from configuration (`appsettings.json` or env var `OPENAI_API_KEY`).
- **Postgres with pgvector** must be available — the init script enables the extension.
- The `Docs/` folder is watched at the configured path; drop in new PDFs and re-ingest.
- Conversation history is per-session (GUID), stored in Postgres for potential future analytics.
- Streaming LLM responses will be used to improve perceived latency in the UI.

- Splits text into overlapping chunks (~500 tokens, ~100 token overlap)
- Generates embeddings via OpenAI
- Upserts chunks + embeddings into `document_chunks`

### 4. Embedding & retrieval service
- `EmbeddingService`: wraps the OpenAI embeddings API
- `RetrievalService`: takes a user query, generates its embedding, performs cosine-similarity search against `document_chunks` in Postgres, returns top-K relevant chunks

### 5. Chat service (RAG orchestration)
- `ChatService`: orchestrates the RAG flow
  - Accepts user message
  - Calls `RetrievalService` to get relevant context
  - Builds a system prompt with retrieved chunks
  - Sends conversation + context to LLM via `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`
  - Persists conversation in `chat_history`

### 6. Blazor Server UI
- Add Blazor Server services to `Program.cs`
- `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` — app shell
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` — chat page with:
  - Scrollable message list (user/assistant bubbles)
  - Text input + send button
  - Loading indicator while awaiting response
- `_Imports.razor`, `App.razor`, `Routes.razor` — standard Blazor scaffolding
- Basic CSS for chat appearance (in `wwwroot/css/`)

### 7. Configuration & Program.cs wiring
- `appsettings.json`: Postgres connection string, OpenAI API key, docs folder path, model settings
- Register all services in DI
- Add app middleware (static files, antiforgery, map Razor components)

### 8. Admin: document re-ingestion endpoint
- Add a simple `/admin/ingest` page or button that triggers re-ingestion of the `Docs/` folder so new PDFs can be loaded without restarting.

## Notes
- **OpenAI API key** required — reads from config (`appsettings.json` or env var `OPENAI_API_KEY`).
- **Postgres with pgvector** must be available — the init script enables the extension.
- The `Docs/` folder is watched at the configured path; drop in new PDFs and re-ingest.
- Conversation history is per-session (GUID), stored in Postgres for potential future analytics.
- Streaming LLM responses will be used to improve perceived latency in the UI.

{"file_text": "# HelpChatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using **C# / Blazor Server** that lets customers ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. Uses **PostgreSQL** for data storage and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

## Approach: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
Extract text from PDFs, chunk it, generate embeddings, store them in Postgres (via `pgvector`), and retrieve relevant chunks at query time to augment LLM prompts.

## Architecture

```
User ⇄ Blazor Server UI ⇄ ChatService ⇄ LLM (OpenAI / Azure OpenAI)
                                ↕
                        EmbeddingService
                                ↕
                      PostgreSQL + pgvector
                                ↕
                      PdfIngestionService ← Docs/*.pdf
```

## Tech Stack
| Component | Technology |
|---|---|
| Framework | .NET 10 / Blazor Server |
| LLM Client | Microsoft.Extensions.AI + OpenAI provider |
| PDF Parsing | UglyToad.PdfPig |
| Embeddings | OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` |
| Vector Store | PostgreSQL + pgvector (via Npgsql) |
| ORM / DB | Npgsql + Dapper (lightweight) |
| UI | Blazor Server components |

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & NuGet packages
Add required NuGet packages: `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI`, `UglyToad.PdfPig`, `Npgsql`, `Dapper`, Blazor Server services.

### 2. Database schema & setup
- Create an init SQL script for Postgres:
  - `pgvector` extension
  - `document_chunks` table: `id`, `file_name`, `chunk_index`, `content` (text), `embedding` (vector(1536)), `created_at`
  - `chat_history` table: `id`, `session_id`, `role`, `content`, `created_at`

### 3. PDF ingestion service
- `PdfIngestionService`: reads all PDFs from `Docs/` folder
- Extracts text page-by-page using PdfPig
- Splits text into overlapping chunks (~500 tokens, ~100 token overlap)
- Generates embeddings via OpenAI
- Upserts chunks + embeddings into `document_chunks`

### 4. Embedding & retrieval service
- `EmbeddingService`: wraps the OpenAI embeddings API
- `RetrievalService`: takes a user query, generates its embedding, performs cosine-similarity search against `document_chunks` in Postgres, returns top-K relevant chunks

### 5. Chat service (RAG orchestration)
- `ChatService`: orchestrates the RAG flow
  - Accepts user message
  - Calls `RetrievalService` to get relevant context
  - Builds a system prompt with retrieved chunks
  - Sends conversation + context to LLM via `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`
  - Persists conversation in `chat_history`

### 6. Blazor Server UI
- Add Blazor Server services to `Program.cs`
- `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` — app shell
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` — chat page with:
  - Scrollable message list (user/assistant bubbles)
  - Text input + send button
  - Loading indicator while awaiting response
- `_Imports.razor`, `App.razor`, `Routes.razor` — standard Blazor scaffolding
- Basic CSS for chat appearance (in `wwwroot/css/`)

### 7. Configuration & Program.cs wiring
- `appsettings.json`: Postgres connection string, OpenAI API key, docs folder path, model settings
- Register all services in DI
- Add app middleware (static files, antiforgery, map Razor components)

### 8. Admin: document re-ingestion endpoint
- Add a simple `/admin/ingest` page or button that triggers re-ingestion of the `Docs/` folder so new PDFs can be loaded without restarting.

## Notes
- **OpenAI API key** required — reads from config (`appsettings.json` or env var `OPENAI_API_KEY`).
- **Postgres with pgvector** must be available — the init script enables the extension.
- The `Docs/` folder is watched at the configured path; drop in new PDFs and re-ingest.
- Conversation history is per-session (GUID), stored in Postgres for potential future analytics.
- Streaming LLM responses will be used to improve perceived latency in the UI.

# HelpChatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using **C# / Blazor Server** that lets customers ask questions about internal product PDF documentation. Uses **PostgreSQL** for data storage and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

## Approach: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
Extract text from PDFs, chunk it, generate embeddings, store them in Postgres (via `pgvector`), and retrieve relevant chunks at query time to augment LLM prompts.

## Architecture

```
User ⇄ Blazor Server UI ⇄ ChatService ⇄ LLM (OpenAI / Azure OpenAI)
                                ↕
                        EmbeddingService
                                ↕
                      PostgreSQL + pgvector
                                ↕
                      PdfIngestionService ← Docs/*.pdf
```

## Tech Stack
| Component | Technology |
|---|---|
| Framework | .NET 10 / Blazor Server |
| LLM Client | Microsoft.Extensions.AI + OpenAI provider |
| PDF Parsing | UglyToad.PdfPig |
| Embeddings | OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` |
| Vector Store | PostgreSQL + pgvector (via Npgsql) |
| ORM / DB | Npgsql + Dapper (lightweight) |
| UI | Blazor Server components |

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & NuGet packages
Add required NuGet packages: `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI`, `UglyToad.PdfPig`, `Npgsql`, `Dapper`, Blazor Server services.

### 2. Database schema & setup
- Create an init SQL script for Postgres:
  - `pgvector` extension
  - `document_chunks` table: `id`, `file_name`, `chunk_index`, `content` (text), `embedding` (vector(1536)), `created_at`
  - `chat_history` table: `id`, `session_id`, `role`, `content`, `created_at`

### 3. PDF ingestion service
- `PdfIngestionService`: reads all PDFs from `Docs/` folder
- Extracts text page-by-page using PdfPig
- Splits text into overlapping chunks (~500 tokens, ~100 token overlap)
- Generates embeddings via OpenAI
- Upserts chunks + embeddings into `document_chunks`

### 4. Embedding & retrieval service
- `EmbeddingService`: wraps the OpenAI embeddings API
- `RetrievalService`: takes a user query, generates its embedding, performs cosine-similarity search against `document_chunks` in Postgres, returns top-K relevant chunks

### 5. Chat service (RAG orchestration)
- `ChatService`: orchestrates the RAG flow

  - Calls `RetrievalService` to get relevant context

  - Sends conversation + context to LLM via `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`
  - Persists conversation in `chat_history`

### 6. Blazor Server UI
- Add Blazor Server services to `Program.cs`
- `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` — app shell
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` — chat page with:
  - Scrollable message list (user/assistant bubbles)

  - Loading indicator while awaiting response
- `_Imports.razor`, `App.razor`, `Routes.razor` — standard Blazor scaffolding
- Basic CSS for chat appearance (in `wwwroot/css/`)

### 7. Configuration & Program.cs wiring
- `appsettings.json`: Postgres connection string, OpenAI API key, docs folder path, model settings
- Register all services in DI
- Add app middleware (static files, antiforgery, map Razor components)

### 8. Admin: document re-ingestion endpoint
- Add a simple `/admin/ingest` page or button that triggers re-ingestion of the `Docs/` folder so new PDFs can be loaded without restarting.

## Notes
- **OpenAI API key** required — reads from config (`appsettings.json` or env var `OPENAI_API_KEY`).
- **Postgres with pgvector** must be available — the init script enables the extension.
- The `Docs/` folder is watched at the configured path; drop in new PDFs and re-ingest.
- Conversation history is per-session (GUID), stored in Postgres for potential future analytics.
- Streaming LLM responses will be used to improve perceived latency in the UI.

```
                                ↕
                        EmbeddingService
                                ↕

                                ↕
                      PdfIngestionService ← Docs/*.pdf
```

| Component | Technology |
|---|---|
| Framework | .NET 10 / Blazor Server |
| LLM Client | Microsoft.Extensions.AI + OpenAI provider |
| PDF Parsing | UglyToad.PdfPig |
| Embeddings | OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` |
| Vector Store | PostgreSQL + pgvector (via Npgsql) |
| ORM / DB | Npgsql + Dapper (lightweight) |

- Register all services in DI
- Add app middleware (static files, antiforgery, map Razor components)

### 8. Admin: document re-ingestion endpoint
- Add a simple `/admin/ingest` page or button that triggers re-ingestion of the `Docs/` folder so new PDFs can be loaded without restarting.

- **OpenAI API key** required — reads from config (`appsettings.json` or env var `OPENAI_API_KEY`).
